Styles › Mediterranean Contemporary

1980s – present · Mediterranean · Global

Mediterranean Contemporary

Warm, relaxed, timeless — stucco walls and clean forms.

  • Overview
  • Design Philosophy
  • Visual Characteristics
  • Materials
  • Color Palette
  • Façade Guide
  • Lighting
  • Building Types
  • Design Checklist
  • Common Mistakes
  • AI Prompts
  • Gallery
  • Related

01 — Overview

Overview

Definition. Mediterranean Modern reinterprets the sun-washed coastal villa — white or sand stucco walls, clean cubic forms, arched or framed openings and shaded outdoor rooms tuned to a warm climate.

Historical background. It distils traditional Mediterranean building (thick walls, courtyards, shade) into a clean, contemporary, indoor–outdoor language for warm regions.

Origin. Spain, Italy, Greece and the wider Mediterranean; global in warm climates.

Timeline

1980

Modern coastal villas refine the vernacular

2000

Indoor–outdoor luxury villa boom

2015

Global warm-climate residential standard

Key Architects

Mediterranean residential practicesRegional villa specialists

Influential Projects

Can Lis

Jørn Utzon · 1971
Mallorca, Spain

Hillside whitewashed house

Hillside whitewashed house

— · —
Aegean

Courtyard villa

Courtyard villa

— · —
Southern Spain

02 — Design Philosophy

Design Philosophy

Core Principles

Response to sun & heatIndoor–outdoor livingThick protective wallsCourtyards & shadeTimeless warmth

The Thinking

Clean white masses, deep shade and seamless terraces make warm-climate living gracious; the exterior is shaped by sun, breeze and the sea.

Values It Represents

RelaxationHospitalityClimate comfortTimelessness

Why It Emerged

A modern answer to the enduring logic of Mediterranean shelter — mass, shade and the outdoor room.

03 — Visual Characteristics

Visual Characteristics

MassingClean white cubic volumes stepping with the terrain; courtyards carved within.
Form LanguageSimple stucco masses with arches, terraces and pergolas.
CompositionSolid walls punctuated by framed openings and shaded voids.
ProportionHuman, generous, grounded.
GeometryOrthogonal with arched and rounded accents.
RhythmRepeated openings, arches and pergola beams.
SymmetryOften asymmetrical, organised around courtyards.
ScaleDomestic and gracious.
Roof TypesFlat terraces or low clay-tiled pitches.
Window ProportionDeep-set openings, arches, shaded loggias; framed views to the sea.
EntranceThrough a shaded threshold or courtyard.
Façade ArticulationWhite/sand stucco, stone trim, clay tile, timber pergolas and deep shade.

04 — Materials

Materials

Stucco / lime render

Reflective white/sand walls that stay cool and timeless.

Natural stone

Local stone for base, trim and walls.

Clay tile

Terracotta roofs of the region.

Timber

Pergolas, shutters and shading.

05 — Color Palette

Color Palette

Warm, sun-bleached, relaxed — earthy neutrals with sea-blue accents.

Primary

Whitewash
#efe9dd
Sand Stucco
#dbcab0

Accent

Terracotta
#b5633a
Aegean Blue
#2f6f8c
Olive
#6c7048

Material Colors

Stone
#c9b79a
Timber
#b78d57

Typical Combinations

  • +White + terracotta + timber
  • +Whitewash + Aegean blue

06 — Façade Design Guide

Façade Design Guide

Window CompositionDeep-set framed openings and arches; shutters and loggias for shade.
EmphasisSolid-wall dominant with carved shaded voids.
Shading DevicesPergolas, deep reveals, loggias and brise-soleil.
BalconiesStone-edged terraces and shaded loggias for outdoor living.
FramesTimber shutters and stone surrounds.
OpeningsGenerous toward views, protected from sun.
Architectural DetailsArches, stone trim, pergola beams, lime-plaster texture.
OrnamentationMinimal — arches, shadow and material warmth provide character.

07 — Lighting

Lighting

Natural Lighting Strategy

Bright reflective white surfaces; deep shade for relief; framed sea light.

Artificial Lighting Concept

Warm, soft, layered; lanterns and uplit textured walls.

Night Façade Appearance

Warm glow on white stucco; lit pergolas and terraces.

Recommendations

Warm 2700K; graze textured plaster; light pergolas and outdoor rooms.

08 — Typical Building Types

Typical Building Types

Where this style performs best:

VillasResorts & hotelsBeach housesRestaurantsBoutique residentialWineries

09 — Design Checklist

Design Checklist

Tap an item to mark it complete — saved to your browser.

  • Use white/sand stucco masses
  • Carve courtyards and shaded terraces
  • Add pergolas and deep loggias
  • Frame openings toward views, shade from sun
  • Use stone trim and clay tile
  • Keep clean cubic forms with arched accents
  • Apply warm earthy palette with blue accents
  • Blend indoor and outdoor living

10 — Common Mistakes

Common Mistakes

Elements that contradict the style — what to avoid:

  • ✕Over-ornate 'faux-Tuscan' pastiche
  • ✕Unshaded glazing in a hot climate
  • ✕Cold grey palettes that deny the warmth
  • ✕Ignoring courtyards and outdoor rooms
  • ✕Heavy dark masses absorbing heat

11 — AI Prompt Examples

AI Prompt Examples

Optimised for Midjourney, ChatGPT image tools or Claude — exterior renders. Tap copy to use.

Exterior

Mediterranean modern villa exterior, white stucco cubic volumes, arched openings, timber pergola, stone terrace, sea view, warm sunlight, architectural photography --ar 3:2 --style raw

Façade Concept

Mediterranean modern façade, whitewashed stucco, deep-set arched openings, timber shutters, stone trim, pergola shade, frontal elevation, warm light

Night View

Mediterranean modern villa exterior at night, warm glow on white stucco, lit pergola terrace, sea beyond, architectural night photography

Aerial View

Aerial of a Mediterranean modern villa, white volumes stepping down to the sea, courtyards, pool, terracotta accents, drone photography

Street Perspective

Street view of a Mediterranean modern house exterior, whitewashed walls, arched gate, bougainvillea, warm light, documentary photography

Masterplan

Mediterranean modern resort masterplan, white courtyard villas stepping to coast, shaded streets, pools, axonometric, warm palette
